goal: Replace weighted/gain-based reinforcement with elimination-based survivability gating aligned with v15 selection law (no weights).

principle.to: reinforce only what survives procedural tests (admissibility, persistence, invariance, trace coherence, loss awareness)

native_platform/residue_phase_continuati: remove or ignore scalar beta/gain for reinforcement decisions
add survivability evaluation before storing trace or applying feedback
apply groove/trace feedback only when survivor == true
native_platform/groove_router.py: update reinforce_active to require survivor == true
do not update groove centroid/op_histogram unless survivor
native_platform/signal_layer.py: compute X channel as cross-view consistency / projection-loss proxy
expose X to continuation for survivability checks

tests.admissibility: candidate produced by allowed operator O*
tests.persistence: mismatch <= running_mismatch_mean OR non-increasing trend over last k steps
tests.cross_view_consistency: X indicates moderate/high consistency (not high disagreement)

continuation_rule_update.before: phi_next = normalize(local + beta_trace * trace + beta_groove * groove)

groove_update_rule.reinforce: update centroid_phi and op_histogram using current segment
groove_update_rule.hold: do not update centroid, increment observation count only
groove_update_rule.reject: no update, optionally increment rejection counter

X_channel_usage.definition: encodes cross-view disagreement / projection loss proxy

expected_results.T2: B mismatch remains higher than A
global: reinforcement only applied to stable segments

failure_signals: no difference between reinforce and hold behavior
final_instruction: Implement survivability-gated reinforcement replacing gain-based updates. Reinforce only segments that pass elimination-based tests derived from v15 (no weights, no scalar optimization).
